(a) A peace officer requesting a person to submit a specimen under Section 522.102 (Implied Consent to Taking of Specimen) shall warn the person that a refusal to submit a specimen will result in the person’s being immediately placed out of service for 24 hours and being disqualified from driving a commercial motor vehicle for at least one year under Section 522.081 (Disqualification) .

(b) A peace officer requesting a person to submit a specimen under Section 522.102 (Implied Consent to Taking of Specimen) is not required to comply with Section 724.015 (Information Provided by Officer Before Requesting Specimen; Statement of Consent) .
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 522. Commercial Driver's Licenses
##### § 522.103 — Warning by Peace Officer

(a) A peace officer requesting a person to submit a specimen under Section 522.102 (Implied Consent to Taking of Specimen) shall warn the person that a refusal to submit a specimen will result in the person’s being immediately placed out of service for 24 hours and being disqualified from driving a commercial motor vehicle for at least one year under Section 522.081 (Disqualification) .

(b) A peace officer requesting a person to submit a specimen under Section 522.102 (Implied Consent to Taking of Specimen) is not required to comply with Section 724.015 (Information Provided by Officer Before Requesting Specimen; Statement of Consent) .

*Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by Acts 2001, 77th Leg., ch. 941, Sec. 13, eff. Sept. 1, 2001.*

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: A peace officer requesting a person to submit a specimen under Section 522.102 (Implied Consent to Taking of Specimen) is not required to comply with Section 724.015 (Information Provided by Officer Before Requesting Specimen; Statement of Consent) .
